response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, performance architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience with chip design, deep learning workload optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architectures, or the advanced degree typically expected for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Broad multi-language engineering background including C/C++ and low-latency systems work
Some exposure to embedded systems and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, NI LabWindows/CVI)
Senior-level tenure at major tech companies with strong software craftsmanship discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or AI accelerator experience
No demonstrated background in deep learning workload analysis, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD
Educational background (BS only) and experience domain do not match a Principal Performance Architect profile

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
